message:
test(fuzz): add symbol_envelope_decrypt fuzz target (resolves br-9seey)

raptorq_envelope_decrypt fuzzes the higher-level
fcp_raptorq::SymbolEnvelope::decrypt wrapper, but the lower-level
primitives in crates/fcp-protocol/src/symbol_envelope.rs
(decrypt_symbol, derive_sender_subkey, encrypt_symbol) were uncovered.
Those are the raw AEAD operations the wrapper composes; an attacker
that synthesizes a frame can reach them directly.

The new harness exercises:
  1. Identity round-trip — encrypt + decrypt restores plaintext.
  2. Tampered ciphertext / auth-tag bit flip — must surface
     SymbolEnvelopeError::DecryptFailed (or CiphertextTooShort on
     truncation), never silently accept.
  3. Wrong sender id — sender_node_id binding via derive_sender_subkey
     means a different sender's subkey must fail authentication.
  4. Wrong zone key — distinct AeadKey must fail authentication.
  5. derive_sender_subkey panic-freedom — the docstring's
     "# Panics: should never happen for 32-byte output" claim is
     pinned by feeding adversarial TailscaleNodeId strings (via
     try_new with canonicalization caps) into the HKDF Info field.

cargo +nightly fuzz build fuzz_symbol_envelope_decrypt: clean build,
no warnings. Not run for hours per dispatch SLA — this commit
verifies build + structural invariants only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
